scope: Catalytic-frontier mechanism implementations, builds, tests, live executions, controls, results, and blockers. Routine read-only navigation and file-edit transport are excluded.
result_policy: This ledger records attempts; lab/results.jsonl remains the authority for measured scientific result claims.

mechanism: CAT_CAS and Main Primers trajectory reconstruction
intervention: Read all Main Primers and relevant CAT_CAS mechanisms; reconcile with Neo3000 mandatory entry documents.
execution: read-only
outcome: Repository trajectory aligns with retained executable state, exact identity, borrow-transform-extract-restore, and bounded carriers, but finite cache reuse alone does not establish unbounded catalytic inference.

mechanism: bounded named in-memory hybrid-state root tick-2 discriminator
execution: Task-A, root-save, catalytic tick 1, root-restore, catalytic tick 2, root-restore, fresh direct tick 2, root-restore, catalytic tick-2 replay, final root-restore; failure assertion preceded explicit erase and process teardown destroyed the root.

mechanism: bounded named in-memory hybrid-state root with complete result and verbose trace
execution: Same RAM-root v1 discriminator as attempt 0016; negative results preserved, final restore and explicit erase required, verbose server trace copied after clean retirement.
trace_diagnosis: Every restored catalytic route admitted exactly 285 cached root tokens and removed memory from position 285 onward. The global prompt cache did not replace the root and no checkpoint fallback restored a branch state. The wrong tick-1 answer hash is deterministic after repeated restores.
next_boundary: Ask tick 2 from the untouched live root before any branch or restore; if it is already C, the retained prefix state itself is insufficient, while live D and restored C would isolate serialization/restore.

intervention: Ask tick 2 from the untouched live RAM root before any branch or restore, then compare the identical token array after restore and under a fresh direct control without changing the server mechanism.

execution: Task-A, root-save, untouched live tick 2, root-restore, tick 1, root-restore, restored tick 2, root-restore, fresh direct tick 2, root-restore, restored tick-2 replay, final root-restore, explicit erase, clean sidecar retirement.
diagnosis: Serialization and restore are not causal for this failure: the untouched live 285-token prefix state already produces the same deterministic wrong C as every restored route, while a full recomputation of the identical 369-token array produces D.
next_boundary: Inspect the exact retained/evaluated tail boundary, then test recomputing exactly one retained tail token as the next single causal intervention if the trace supports it.

execution: Inspect the preserved 87e764c verbose trace and controller invariants; no server, model, or file-state mutation.

outcome.interpretation: The 285-token end-of-generation live state is not an exact executable prefix for this Qwen35MoE route. The narrower cause remains unresolved between autoregressively generated-tail boundary state and broader hybrid prefill-versus-generation path dependence.

rejected_next_intervention: An in-place one-token trim to 284 is unsafe because token-count rollback cannot produce a matching recurrent state when n_rs_seq is zero.
next_boundary: Build a 270-token prompt-only root using a cache-disabled zero-output request, then re-evaluate the 15 known Task-A generated tokens plus the unchanged 84-token continuation and compare with the same fresh direct control.

mechanism: sustained N=16 prompt-only native RAM root

mechanism: sustained N=16 prompt-only native RAM root
intervention: Add a streaming controller that saves one fresh 270-token prompt-only native RAM root, restores it before each distinct catalytic branch, re-evaluates the known 15-token generated tail, runs same-token cache-disabled direct controls, checks exact generated-token digests at N=2/4/8/16, closes the root, and explicitly erases it.

execution.initial_scaffold: Copied the reviewed file-carrier sustained controller and tests as two new untracked files, then converted only the carrier lifecycle and accounting.
execution.caught_before_execution: Full-file inspection found an incomplete generated-token digest call left during conversion; syntax was repaired before any live contact.
execution.patch_dry_runs: Three malformed unified-diff hunk counts were rejected by git apply --check before mutation; corrected patches then applied cleanly.
execution.negative_outcome_preservation: Catalytic or direct wrong answers now produce a complete reject record rather than an early exception; structural cache, identity, input-token, and evidence failures still stop the run.
execution.accounting: Fresh-token and wall-time prefixes include Task-A, fresh prompt-root materialization, save, catalytic branches, closure probes, restores, same-token direct controls, and final restore plus erase at executed N. Resource samples, root bytes, restore bytes, controller growth, and WDDM telemetry remain explicit.

outcome: Controller and pure acceptance tests are ready for pre-live identity commit; no model contact occurred.

outcome.interpretation: The task family is too weak for a utility-preserving N16 acceptance because the fresh model itself fails seven ticks. Independently, tick 11 is a valid carrier discriminator because fresh direct is correct while restored prompt-root reuse is wrong on the identical token array.

next_boundary: Run a narrow tick-11 live/restored/direct/replay prompt-root discriminator to distinguish prompt-prefill path divergence from RAM serialization or repeated-restore effects.

mechanism: neo-exp-0053 N16 evidence and tick-11 localization

mechanism: neo-exp-0053 compact record and truth-synchronization review

outcome.metric_reproduction: matched the canonical N=2/4/8/16 token, wall, utility, root, resource, artifact, and cleanup values
outcome.recommendation: Preserve the general equal-utility claim ceiling at N8, record N16 engineering/root amortization separately from the utility rejection, localize tick11 before replacing the failed rotor utility family.

next_boundary: Complete the already-selected tick-11 live/restored/direct/replay discriminator; treat task-family replacement as a later separate intervention.

execution: A user-authorized read-only lane searched the CAT_CAS lab and Main Primers for mechanisms relevant to identical-token prefix divergence. It made no file, model, server, hardware, branch, or Git mutation.

outcome.runtime_geometry_hypothesis: The 270-token root crosses n_ubatch=128 as 128+128+14, whereas the fresh approximately 370-token tick-11 prefill continues as 128+128+approximately114. Fused recurrent GDN loads and writes recurrent state at invocation boundaries, so segmentation is a hidden state coordinate not represented by submitted-token identity.
outcome.smallest_post_localization_mechanism: If untouched live, restored, and replay tick11 all disagree with fresh direct, move only the root boundary from 270 to 256 tokens so catalytic and fresh routes share the first two complete 128-token ubatches; replay the remaining 14 static prompt tokens plus the unchanged generated tail and branch suffix.

evidence.finding: same final hash under a wrong schedule retained different physical cache readout
float recomputation broke exact uncompute while raw f32 bits restored it; engine utility remained invalid
active fingerprinted root-cache speed claim is reported but unreproduced; approximate aliasing must not transfer
compression achieved 8.81x size reduction but only 2 percent token match and degraded output, so it is not the next mechanism
conceptual trajectory and restoration framing only

next_boundary: Complete the unchanged tick-11 live/restored/direct/replay discriminator first; authorize the 256-token aligned root only if it localizes the divergence to live prompt-prefill geometry rather than serialization.

execution: An independent reviewer inspected only the new controller and tests without editing files or contacting the model or servers. It initially blocked live use on missing explicit 11/270/285 pins, protocol-test coverage, overstated operation-wall naming, and an overbroad identity invariant. After fixes, it re-reviewed the files and found no blocking correctness issue.

initial_blockers: tick and root boundaries dynamically admitted drift
operation-only wall labeled total measured run
closed: tick exactly 11 and prompt/completed boundaries exactly 270/285 are hard-gated and negative-tested
restore slot and source metadata match save
wall accounting is split into accounted request/root operations and outer experiment wall envelope
mocked test covers exact cross-call route/restore/erase interleaving, cache flags, numeric accounting, final restore, and accept path

next_boundary: Commit the exact controller identity, then run one tick-11 discriminator against the pinned model and RAM-root binary.

execution.review_repairs: Hard-pinned tick 11 and 270/285 boundaries, strengthened slot metadata checks, added an outer experiment wall envelope, narrowed evidence labels, and added mocked lifecycle/accounting tests.

next_boundary: Commit this exact identity and execute it once with external JSON and verbose trace evidence; do not change task, boundary, seed, sampler, binary, or model.
